summary: Matched a substantial portion of sysdolphin baselib devcom by replacing minimal stubs/globals with real DevCom queue, callback, allocation, and request logic. The PR also tightened related headers and call sites: HSD_DevCom now uses size_t for size, HSD_DevComIsBusy returns bool, HSD_DevComRequest returns an int request id, HSD_AudioMalloc gained a concrete void* HSD_AudioMalloc(size_t) prototype, and lbfile was adjusted to use clearer destination/size/priority parameter names plus a ROUND_UP_32 helper. Symbol data for the former monolithic HSD_DevCom_804083B8 region was split into individual string labels used by devcom assertions/messages.
what_changed: Expanded src/sysdolphin/baselib/devcom.c from a minimal HSD_DevComIsBusy implementation and raw globals into matched DevCom infrastructure: static ARQRequest arrays, DevCom status queues, relay buffers, free-list state, wakeup callbacks, unlink logic, and HSD_DevComRequest.
Changed devComStatus from bool devComStatus[4] to static HSD_DevCom* devComStatus[4], with HSD_DevComIsBusy returning whether a queue pointer is non-null.
Added HSD_DevComUnlink with interrupt disable/restore protection, queue traversal, tail-cache maintenance via HSD_DevCom_804C6330, and an HSD_ASSERT fallback if the target node is not found.

Added HSD_DevComStdCallback and HSD_DevComARAMCallback paths that identify ARQ request slot, clear ARAM/relay state, invoke user callbacks when present, unlink completed requests, return request nodes to the free list, and wake DVD/ARAM processing.

Implemented HSD_DevComRequest to allocate or reuse HSD_DevCom nodes, validate alignment and buffer-size constraints with HSD_ASSERT, normalize priority for non-priority request types, enqueue requests by priority, and return a request id.

Left several DevCom functions as documented nonmatched placeholders using /// #function comments: HSD_DevComARAMWakeUp, HSD_DevComDVDStdCallback, HSD_DevComDVDARAMEndCallback, HSD_DevComDVDMemCallback, HSD_DevComDVDCallback, HSD_DevComDVDWakeUp, and HSD_DevComCancelEx.

Updated devcom.h to include placeholder.h and dolphin/ar.h, move the HSD_DevCom forward declaration to baselib/forward.h, use size_t for HSD_DevCom.size, expose ARQRequest callback prototypes, and correct HSD_DevComRequest to return int.
Added typedef struct HSD_DevCom HSD_DevCom; to src/sysdolphin/baselib/forward.h.
Changed HSD_AudioMalloc in synth.h from UNK_RET HSD_AudioMalloc(UNK_PARAMS) to void* HSD_AudioMalloc(size_t), supporting typed allocation in HSD_DevComRequest.

Adjusted lbFile_800164A4 signature and internals: renamed/retargeted parameters to file, dest, size, pri; wrote file size through size_t*; selected DevCom type from destination address; used HSD_DevComRequest's corrected argument order and return signature.
Added ROUND_UP_32(x) in lbfile.c and reused it for DevCom transfer size and heap allocation alignment instead of open-coded ((*x + 0x1F) & 0xFFFFFFE0).
Simplified gm_801A4014 busy wait from HSD_DevComIsBusy(1) != 0 to HSD_DevComIsBusy(1), reflecting the bool return type.

Split config/GALE01/symbols.txt data at 0x804083B8 from one global HSD_DevCom_804083B8 object into multiple lbl_804083B8 through lbl_80408470 string symbols, likely matching the devcom assertion/message strings.
smart_moves: Converted devComStatus into a pointer queue rather than a bool array; HSD_DevComIsBusy still provides a bool API while preserving the real internal linked-list state needed by matching code.
Used interrupt guards around free-list and queue mutation, which matches low-level asynchronous DVD/ARAM callback expectations and avoids race-prone decomp code.

Introduced a small static inline HSD_DevComARAMCallback_inline helper to isolate the exact free-list reinsertion sequence, a common matching tactic when statement ordering/register allocation matters.
Centralized 32-byte alignment in ROUND_UP_32, improving readability while preserving the bitwise alignment expression needed by file transfer and heap allocation paths.
Corrected HSD_AudioMalloc's prototype before using it for typed HSD_DevCom block allocation, avoiding casts or placeholder signatures that would obscure matching.
Kept unknown or not-yet-matched DevCom routines as explicit /// #function placeholders instead of overclaiming implementation, while adding prototypes so matched functions can call them.
Split the data symbol block into individual strings, which is useful when assertion strings or callback labels must be referenced independently by matched C.
decomp_lessons: When a public function appears to return a simple status, inspect nearby queue usage before modeling the backing storage as bool; here the status array is really per-priority HSD_DevCom* queue heads.
For asynchronous subsystems, matching often depends on modeling queue heads, queue tails, free lists, and interrupt masking explicitly rather than using simplified containers or booleans.

Return types can unlock cleaner callers: changing HSD_DevComRequest from void to int reflects the request id produced from HSD_DevCom_804D6050 + priority and clarifies cancellation/tracking semantics.
Use size_t for byte counts when the struct field and API represent transfer sizes; this propagated from HSD_DevCom.size into HSD_DevComRequest and lbFile_800164A4.
Alignment invariants should be expressed at the boundary of the request API. HSD_DevComRequest asserts src, dest, and size are all 32-byte aligned and size is nonzero.
Symbol-map granularity matters for matching data references: a single large data object may need to be decomposed into string labels once code starts referencing individual messages.
Forward declarations should live in shared forward headers when multiple baselib headers need the type; devcom.h now relies on baselib/forward.h for HSD_DevCom.

If a function's decomp is incomplete, add accurate prototypes for related unknown functions rather than forcing an implementation. This preserves compile/link structure and supports incremental matching.
naming_conventions: DevCom globals use existing address-derived placeholder names when semantic names are not yet known, such as HSD_DevCom_804C6330, HSD_DevCom_804D77F0, HSD_DevCom_804D7808, and HSD_DevCom_804D6050.
Local variables in newly matched code still use decompiler-style names when meaning is uncertain, such as temp_r30, temp_r3, var_r0, and var_r5.
Constants introduced for discovered semantics use uppercase macro style: DEVCOMDEST_SBUF, DEVCOM_BUF_SIZE, INIT_N_DEVCOMS, and ROUND_UP_32.
Function names follow established HSD_DevCom subsystem prefixes: HSD_DevComIsBusy, HSD_DevComUnlink, HSD_DevComStdCallback, HSD_DevComARAMCallback, HSD_DevComRequest.
Unknown data strings in symbols.txt remain address labels with lbl_ prefixes rather than speculative semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used static inline helpers for small logic fragments, specifically HSD_DevComARAMCallback_inline and HSD_DevComGetDestType, likely to preserve instruction shape without exporting extra symbols.
Used gotos and explicit cleanup labels in HSD_DevComUnlink to model original control flow around OSRestoreInterrupts.
Used exact bitmask operations for destination type and priority classification: type & 7 for destination type and (type & 0x38) == 0x20 for priority preservation.
Modeled request ids as a monotonically increasing base incremented by 4 plus priority: result = HSD_DevCom_804D6050 + pri; HSD_DevCom_804D6050 += 4.
Used pointer identity comparisons against devComARQR[0][0] and devComARQR[1][0] to recover the ARQ channel index.
Allocated HSD_DevCom nodes in batches of 16 and linked dc[1] onward into a free list, matching the original allocator pattern rather than allocating one node at a time.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that implement HSD_DevComARAMWakeUp or HSD_DevComDVDWakeUp after the placeholders added in pr-1600.
Search for later renames of HSD_DevCom_804C6330, HSD_DevCom_804D77F0, HSD_DevCom_804D7808, or HSD_DevCom_804D6050 to recover semantic global names.
Check whether HSD_DevComCancelEx later uses the int request id returned by HSD_DevComRequest.
Look for follow-up cleanup of lbFile_800164A4 parameter names and HSD_DevComCallback signature once more DevCom code is matched.
Search for later symbol-map changes around lbl_804083B8 through lbl_80408470 to identify the assertion strings semantically.
